en_body:
Morning comes very ordinarily. And because all ordinary things in this life come and go very regularly, calmly, and silently: Therefore only open the eyes, and it is daylight.

 There are people sleeping around me. So many. They lie scattered upon the floor, upon the sofa...with little need of pillows, blankets. Their drunken breathing may be because of weariness or because they sleep very well; I cannot know for certain, I only know they appear to be sleeping.

 Just as life is happening upon the surface of this earth, the life of a new day or old night of the beings around this blue planet, where I too am dwelling: I too do not know how they are living or dying. I know only they are breathing or not.

 As for me. Me, you ask? I too do not understand what I am doing anymore!!! Surrealism...can it be that these things far too vague...Far too distant, too fantastical there are very real within my first current?

 I am living, I know this, because I hear myself breathing: but my soul, something no one knows for certain what it is...even students of the sciences and theology: it wishes to uproot itself, wishes to abandon me. Something sacred because it gives my body the nourishment of Life: Yet, it no longer embraces passionately this trap, this bodily prison, or thirsts for the substance and savor of life.

 What are these days and months now becoming??
 Where am I going??
 What am I doing??
 How can I feel the
 TRUTH
 that is governing all things now imagining themselves to be me??
note: The poem's “soul” is poised between theological mystery and Buddhist estrangement from bodily attachment.
